message:
fix(fcp-host): dedicate RPC dispatcher task + hard 10s I/O timeouts on connector subprocess

Two long-running reliability bugs in the host → connector subprocess
bridge:

1. SubprocessConnector held `Mutex<ConnectorProcessRunner>` and took
   the lock for the full duration of each RPC. The runner owns a
   single stdin/stdout pair so the serialization is necessary, but
   doing it inside `self.resilience.execute(...)` means the retry /
   backoff layer held the runner lock across await points — any
   in-flight RPC that stalled also froze the resilience state machine
   for that connector and blocked every other caller waiting on the
   same mutex instead of failing fast.

2. send_json / read_json both awaited on stdin.write_all + flush and
   stdout.read_line with no timeout. A connector that accepted input
   but stopped emitting a line (crash between parse and response,
   deadlock, infinite loop) would hang the host RPC indefinitely.

Replace the Mutex with an mpsc-channel RPC dispatcher pattern:

- Spawn a dedicated task at construction that owns the runner and
  drains `mpsc::Receiver<ConnectorRpcRequest>`. Each request carries
  a oneshot::Sender<io::Result<Value>> so the caller wakes up as soon
  as the runner completes or errors.
- request() serializes on the mpsc send, not on a held Mutex guard,
  so waiters don't tie up runner state — the dispatcher task always
  makes forward progress.
- Queue capacity bound `CONNECTOR_RPC_QUEUE_CAPACITY = 64` so a
  pathological backlog shows up as back-pressure on the sender
  rather than unbounded memory growth.
- `_runner_task: JoinHandle<()>` keeps the task alive for the
  connector's lifetime; on drop the sender is closed, the task's
  recv() returns None, the loop exits cleanly.

Add explicit timeouts on the child I/O paths:

- `CONNECTOR_RPC_IO_TIMEOUT = 10s`
- `send_json` wraps write_all + write b"\n" + flush in
  fcp_async_core::time::timeout — maps a TimedOut error via
  `connector_io_timeout_error("stdin write")`.
- `read_json` wraps read_line the same way, reporting
  "stdout read timed out after 10s".
- Error surface maps to `HostError::RegistryError(format!("connector IO error: {err}"))`
  at the RPC boundary, so upstream retry/backoff sees a concrete
  failure class instead of a hung future.

Also tighten the handshake lock in `ensure_handshaken`: the zone
check now holds the lock only for the read; the mutation that
assigns `handshaken_zone = Some(zone)` re-locks after the handshake
RPC completes. This removes a hidden `Mutex` held across an await
that previously crossed an unbounded RPC boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
